blocks.text:
A bob of mass $m$ hangs from a light string of length $L$ and is released from a small angle.
Neglecting friction, find the period $T$ of its oscillation.

blocks.caption: The bob swings as a simple pendulum (small-angle SHM).
blocks.source:
pendulum at=(0,3) length=2.6 angle=22 anim=spin anim-about=(0,3) anim-swing=44 anim-period=2
dim from=(0.4,3) to=(0.4,0.4) off=0.0 label="L"

meta.solution:
A small angular displacement gives simple harmonic motion whose angular frequency is the
square root of $g/L$. The period is a full turn divided by that frequency:
$$T = \frac{2\pi}{\sqrt{g/L}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}}.$$
meta.subject: physics
meta.title: Period of a Simple Pendulum
